Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

INTERVENOR STARSTONE NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Ruling On Its Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff Cross Defendant Vince Moreci:

is DENIED because 1) the motion is untimely, and more critically, 2) the moving party has not met the requisite showing of "new or different facts, circumstances, or law," as required by C.C.P. section 1008(a), upon which the Court may "modify, amend, or revoke the prior order." In fact, the only item of information in the moving party's declaration in support of the motion for reconsideration that could be construed as a new fact is in fact a hearsay statement unsupported by the records of the Court that Judge Feng had confirmed that discovery was reopened. The Court's Register of Action shows that no party in this action has moved the Court to reopen or otherwise extend discovery motion deadline pursuant to C.C.P. section 2024.050 between the November 2021 order denying a prior discovery motion as untimely and June 23, 2022, the date of the hearing when the underlying order was issued. There is also no Court order in the records that states discovery was reopened. Even if discovery was somehow reopened between November 2021 and June 2022, it would be an abuse of discretion of the Court unless the elements of a C.C.P. section 2024.050 motion have been presented to the Court and considered by the Court. Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568, 1588 (2008); Cottini v. Enloe Medical Center, 226 Cal. App. 4th 401 (2014).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Disqualification Of All Counsel Of Record For Defendant Scaffold Solutions Inc
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. GARY B. ROTH, Esq. of BOXER & GERSON, LLP (Tel: 510-835-8870) for Plaintiff
Vince Moreci;
Atty. BARRY W. PONTICELLO, Esq. of England Ponticello & St.Clair (Tel: 619-255-6450) for Plaintiff-In-Intervention Starstone National Insurance Company fka Torus National Insurance Company;
Atty. NICHOLAS B. BRUMMEL, Esq. Wolfe & Wyman LLP (Tel: 925-708-7000) for Defendant Scaffold Solutions, Inc.

HEARING RE: INTERVENOR STARSTONE NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Disqualification Of All Counsel Of Record For Defendant Scaffold Solutions Inc.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff-in-intervention Starstone National Insurance Company's motion for disqualification of all counsel of record for defendant Scaffold Solutions Inc. is denied. Intervenor lacks standing to bring this motion. In California state court, the party seeking disqualification must have or have had an attorney-client relationship with the "target" attorney. (See Marriage of Murchison (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 847, 851; see Great Lakes Construction, Inc. v. Burman (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1347, 1356 [a "standing" requirement is implicit in disqualification motions].) Intervenor does not show it had or has an attorney-client relationship with Scaffold Solution's counsel. Intervenor argues that as a non-client with a personal stake in the motion it does have standing to bring the motion pursuant to Colyer v. Smith (C.D. Cal. 1999) 50 F.Supp.2d 966. Colyer's minority view of standing is not binding on this court. (Great Lakes Construction, Inc.,186 Cal.App.4th at 1351.) However, even applying the reasoning in Colyer, Intervenor does not satisfy the two elements. (See Colyer, 50 F.Supp.2d at 973 [must show an "invasion of a legally protected interest which is (a) concrete and particularized, and (b) actual or imminent, not conjectural or hypothetical"].) Intervenor argues that disqualification is proper based on the court's obligation to ensure the fair administration of justice; however, this is not enough to give a non-client standing. (See Colyer, 50 F.Supp.2d at 973 [broad interest in the administration of justice is insufficiently concrete and particularized to support a finding of standing].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order To Motion To Independent Medical Evaluation; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FULTON STREET CONSTRUCTION, INC., ERIK LIU's Motion For Protective Order To Motion To Independent Medical Evaluation; Request For Sanctions.

Granted as follows: Plaintiff shall make himself available on a mutually convenient date within the next three weeks for a medical examination by Dr. Jadali. In conducting the examination Dr. Jadali will not be precluded from asking normal medical questions with regard to the plaintiff's medical history, including the medical history and treatment of plaintiff with regard to the subject injuries, prior injuries to the same body parts and pre-existing injuries to the same body parts claimed in the subject incident, but shall not include a medical history with regard to unrelated injuries to other body parts. Dr. Jadali may question plaintiff with regard to how he allegedly "slipped, tripped and/or fell on scaffolding stairs" from the time that he began falling and thereafter. The area of inquiry shall not include questions about the facts of the incident prior to the beginning of the alleged fall. Abel v. 1979 Union St. Corp., CGC-15-543471 (decided May 31, 2016). As in Abel, it would have been preferable if defendant had sought resolution of the dispute before the initial medical examination, and for that reason defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
